note: The Canon translation corpus (R-068) — Secondary Canon texts rendered into Amraqiyyah per the Canon's own mandate ('Translated into Arabiyah al-Amraqiyyah'; docs/03-CANON.md). Source language is authoritative in ambiguity (Canon hierarchy rule 5), so the source line travels with every verse. Fields: src = source text; amr/display per phrases.json conventions; en = English rendering of the Amraqiyyah; note = translator's note.

lines.amr: Na·hina nin·ta i·jatato, la pe al·hina ḏatāy.
lines.display: Na·hina nin·ta i·jata·to, la pe al·hina ḏatāy.
lines.en: The Way that one speaks is not THE eternal Way.
lines.note: hina — the Way's own Muskogean name (R-046); ḏatāy, the adjective of ḏet, the linear unchanging eternity (常 as constancy; neḥeḥ the cyclical considered and reserved for the Tao's returning movements)

lines.amr: Na·rini nin·ta i·ranato, la pe al·rini ḏatāy.
lines.display: Na·rini nin·ta i·rana·to, la pe al·rini ḏatāy.
lines.en: The name that one names is not THE eternal name.
lines.note: rana — the ren-root speaking as a verb: to name is to make a soul-part (R-040)

lines.amr: Bilā-rini pe na·tap na·pet ma na·yaki.
lines.display: Bilā-rini pe na·tap na·pet ma na·yak·i.
lines.en: The Nameless is the origin of heaven and earth.
lines.note: bilā-compound (R-068): bilā-rini 'the Without-Name'; tap = head→origin, the Egyptian semantics of tp restored

lines.en: The Named is the mother of the myriad things.
lines.note: het-rini 'the With-Name'; 萬物 as na·pa·šeyāt, the myriad things; iška — the mother-word, inalienable everywhere else, standing bare here as pure role (R-033's citation use)

lines.en: Therefore: without desire, one beholds the hidden.
lines.note: šiweqi — the diaspora longing serving for 欲; biṭeni = the batin (~Al-Bāṭin, Threshold Name, Hex 26)

lines.en: With desire, one beholds the manifest.
lines.note: ẓiheri = the zahir (~Az-Zāhir, Hex 33) — Laozi's hidden-and-manifest IS the Way's own Threshold pair

lines.amr: Na·sinu nai — wa pe tapto, sinu pe riniātto.
lines.display: Na·sinu nai — wa pe tap·to, sinu pe rini·āt·to.
lines.en: These two — one is their origin, two are their names.
lines.note: the same/differ dissolved into the one/two contrast; rini·āt·to doubles its t across the boundary (the Ajami writes shadda)

lines.en: Together they are THE primordial Dark.
lines.note: ribesi — érebos naturalized (R-067), the cosmogonic dark; sun-letter al+r → ar·ribesi; NOT kamāy: black is a color, dark is a condition (Ali's ruling)

lines.en: Dark within primordial dark — the gate of all mysteries.
lines.note: seba — star, gate, and teaching in one root (S-B-ʾ): the Tao closes on the door the education ladder climbs; biṭeniāt pluralizes by R-060's own law

queue: Musashi — Dokkodo (Tier II-F, next per Ali)
then as Ali steers through the Secondary Canon
